danger_signals.visual_form: Looney Tunes character sprites (Foghorn Leghorn, Sylvester, Tasmanian Devil, Daffy Duck)
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles blocking path to birthday party; jealous characters actively preventing progress
Direct threat requiring active defense or redirection

danger_signals.visual_form: Absence of visible hearts in HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Approaching game-over state; character vulnerability increasing

safety_signals.meaning: Recovery opportunity; safe to engage or collect before continuing
Non-critical safe pickups; reward for exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: Landing on top of enemies without collision
safety_signals.meaning: Alternative safe movement method; spatial positioning allows damage avoidance

safety_signals.visual_form: Special carrot appearing after Daffy Duck avoidance
safety_signals.meaning: Exit pathway unlocked; progression to next level safe

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Birthday party destination; progression through WB-branded levels

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual character designs (Foghorn Leghorn, Sylvester cat, Tasmanian Devil, Daffy Duck)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier variants at level endings; distinct attack patterns per character
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout levels as standard enemies; positioned at level exits as boss encounters

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: WB Shield logo (regional variant: Japan uses '50' commemoration instead)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Collectible item graphic; title screen branding; game packaging

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bugs Bunny separated from birthday destination; obstacles blocking path
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Protagonist must navigate through enemy-filled stages; friends actively obstruct passage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Looney Tunes characters positioned as intentional blockers; trick-based narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies behave as coordinated obstruction team; minigames at level ends suggest celebration setup

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each level completion advances toward birthday house destination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Minigame access unlocked after boss defeats; extra lives accumulation signals celebration readiness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss characters explicitly designed as famous Looney Tunes personalities; visual recognition of fame disparity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters attack with distinct patterns; some unkillable (Daffy) reinforcing avoidance-based solutions

environmental_storytelling.example: Presence of specific Looney Tunes characters (Foghorn Leghorn, Sylvester, Tasmanian Devil) communicates jealousy theme without dialogue; player recognizes these as supporting cast
Progression structure (multiple stages → boss encounter → birthday house approach) visually communicates party-seeking narrative
Enemies actively blocking path represent friends' jealousy made tangible; mallet combat represents Bugs' effort to overcome obstacles
Carrots as collectible reward directly reference Bugs' canonical food preference; hearts as health restore use universal symbol language

environmental_storytelling.technique: Regional visual variation for cultural context
environmental_storytelling.example: Japanese version features '50' on item graphic (50th birthday anniversary commemoration); international versions replace with WB logo (brand-focused messaging)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mallet-damageable; projectile-based attack pattern requiring defensive play
Mallet-damageable; distinct combat mechanics from other bosses
Cannot be defeated by mallet; requires complete avoidance to reach exit carrot; signals alternative solution method
Defeatable via mallet; can be trampled safely; scattered throughout level terrain
Standard movement and attack speed; mallet weapon accessible
Visual indicator (heart count reduction); heightened vulnerability to enemy contact

notes: This is a thematic platformer centered on a narrative premise (50th birthday party) rather than geopolitical conflict. Danger/safety signals are mechanically straightforward enemy/item based. Faction indicators represent character-driven conflict (jealousy) rather than ideological opposition. World-state manifestation relies on level progression narrative structure and boss encounter positioning. Environmental storytelling uses character recognition and mechanical metaphor. Regional differences show localization adaptation (Japan: anniversary focus via '50' graphic; International: brand-focused WB shield). Unused content (angry Porky Pig intro, cut ending with Marc Antony/Pussyfoot/Road Runner/Yosemite Sam) suggests original design included broader character interaction narrative now absent from final build. Game communicates world-state primarily through level geography and enemy positioning rather than dynamic environmental shifts.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (unused graphics, regional differences, development artifacts)
wikipedia - The Bugs Bunny Birthday Blowout (plot, gameplay mechanics, character roles, boss encounters)
